title: Japanese national football team returns from World Cup and aims for a friendly with Brazil in November
summary: Coach Moriyasu did not confirm his continuity but expressed optimism about the future, while awaiting the appointment of a new coach and a match against Brazil was confirmed.

title: Fire at Tokyo elementary school caused by a teacher drying clothes with an electric heater
summary: The music teacher used a personal heater to dry her clothing inside the classroom, causing a fire without casualties, according to education authorities.

title: Japan and India strengthen strategic alliance in economic security, artificial intelligence, and clean energy
summary: The prime ministers agreed on a private investment of 2 trillion yen and cooperation in semiconductors, critical minerals, and the production of green ammonia.

title: US employment grows only 57,000 jobs in June, well below expectations
summary: The slowdown in job creation reinforces expectations that the Federal Reserve may stop raising interest rates.

title: Russia attacks Ukraine with 496 missiles and drones, leaving at least 20 dead in Kyiv
summary: The massive bombardment hit residential buildings and a hotel in the Ukrainian capital, in one of the most intense attacks in recent months.

title: 11-year-old boy causes accident in Thailand while driving a truck, leaving 8 dead
summary: The minor took the vehicle without authorization and rammed a procession of monks, causing a tragedy that shocked the country.

title: Nippon Ham Fighters set NPB record with four consecutive home runs
summary: The Hokkaido team hit four straight home runs from the first batter in the first inning, achieving a historic comeback.

title: Political stagnation in Japan over seat reduction, alternate capital, and Imperial Household reform
summary: The ruling party and opposition fail to reach agreements while center-left parties explore a merger and the discussion on imperial succession is prioritized.

title: WHO declares end of hantavirus outbreak in Japan after lifting last quarantine
summary: The World Health Organization confirmed the containment of the virus and the end of isolation for the last infected patients.

title: Haruki Murakami publishes 'Natsuho', his first novel in three years
summary: The work, focused on a children's book illustrator, went on sale at midnight with a simpler style, according to the author.

title: Japan's tax revenue reaches a record 84.2 trillion yen in fiscal year 2025
summary: Revenue grew for the sixth consecutive year driven by inflation and wage increases, with increases in consumption and income taxes.

title: Summer bonus at large Japanese companies exceeds one million yen for the first time
summary: According to a Keidanren survey, the average bonus amount reached a historic level, reflecting improved corporate profits.

title: Cockroach remains found in school lunch in Okayama and meal served after removing contaminated part
summary: The incident, which drew criticism, occurred at an elementary school where staff decided to remove the affected area and continue serving.

title: Direct connection project between Nishitetsu Kaizuka Line and Fukuoka subway accelerates
summary: After 30 years of stagnation, the option of six-car trains is being evaluated to alleviate congestion on one of the busiest routes in the country.

title: Yen strengthens to 160 yen per dollar for first time in two weeks
summary: The Japanese currency appreciated due to official intervention and reduced expectations of a rate hike in the United States.

title: 'Kirby' toys recalled from market due to battery explosion risk and defective snacks
summary: Bandai and Bourbon called for the return of thousands of units of their products due to possible safety failures.

title: Controversies on Japanese television over sexist comments and issues with host Seiya
summary: A TV Tokyo program received criticism for misogynistic statements, while comedian Seiya faced controversy over his hosting on another show.

title: Two child deaths shock Japan: elementary school student dies at school and 2-year-old baby dies from abuse
summary: In separate cases, a 10-year-old boy disappeared during class and was found dead, while in Wakayama parents admitted to abusing their son.

title: Manchester City signs Anderson for 25 billion yen and Belgium achieves historic comeback in World Cup
summary: The English club completed the transfer of the midfielder, while the Belgian team staged an epic comeback reminiscent of the 'Miracle of Rostov'.

title: Japan faces adverse weather phenomena: heat wave and linear rain bands
summary: Very high temperatures are expected in large areas of the country, while Kyushu experienced torrential rain and strong winds that caused damage.
